reasoning: Kentucky straight, 70/21/9 corn/rye/malted barley. 21% rye is mid-high but not a 'high-rye' bourbon. Reposado tequila cask finish adds agave-fruit notes. 45% ABV, 3+ yr — youngish, lighter body.

Blend dominated by a 60/36/4 Kentucky 10.7-yr bourbon — 36% rye is HIGH-rye, putting spice at 3. Long aging gives heavy oak and developed esters. Lower corn percentage means less candy-sweet. 47% ABV.

Wheated bourbon (51/45/4 corn/wheat/barley) — zero rye means zero rye-spice. Bauman's Cider barrel finish + explicit apple/toffee/brown sugar tasting notes push sweet and fruit to 3. 100 proof = full body.

ba_name: Mountain Summit Single Barrel Straight Bourbon
reasoning: 68/18/8/6 corn/rye/wheat/barley. Detailed tasting notes (caramel, butterscotch, vanilla, molasses, tobacco, leather, cherry, stewed fruit). Utah climate extreme wood extraction = high oak. 47% ABV.

75/21/4 mash, 4 yr new charred oak, Napa Cabernet finish. Tasting notes: brown sugar, caramel, pink flowers, milk chocolate, hazelnut, baked apple, sugared berries, oak. Wine finish drives fruit to 3. 44% ABV is on the lighter side.

ba_name: Stiefel's Select Single Barrel Four Grain Bourbon
reasoning: Four-grain (corn/wheat/rye/barley) Washington bourbon, single barrel, 4 yr heavy char. No tasting notes available — scored from mash construction alone. Wheat softens, heavy char drives oak up. Re-score after tasting.

ba_name: Tamworth Crab Trapper Green Crab Flavored Whiskey
reasoning: 4-yr bourbon steeped with green crab stock and a low-country-boil spice blend. NOT a substitution candidate for classic bourbon cocktails — the brininess and savory spice dominate over the underlying bourbon. Scored as the actual flavor experience, not the base bourbon: heavy on spice (boil seasoning), low on sweet/oak/vanilla/fruit (overridden by brine).
